title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation

jokes.text: Wait a minute, I'm at work. You gotta pay for it.
jokes.setup: Moe initially offers Homer free beer from the fridge
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization he's at work and needs to charge
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy, plays on Moe's confusion between home and work

jokes.text: Series of notes from family members taking Homer's money
jokes.setup: Homer looks for his money
jokes.punchline: Multiple family members have left increasingly desperate notes explaining why they took his money
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane (diapers) to threatening (leg breaking). Perfect visual gag structure.

jokes.text: Fine. There are plenty other ways for me to alter my consciousness.
jokes.setup: Homer can't afford alcohol
jokes.punchline: Homer's determination to get intoxicated by any means necessary
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic - sophisticated vocabulary for simple desire to get wasted

jokes.text: Are you gonna buy those toads or just lick them? Lick them. Go away.
jokes.setup: Homer trying alternative consciousness alteration
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission he's just going to lick psychedelic toads
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery, absurd situation handled matter-of-factly, excellent economy

jokes.text: Thanks for the donation, Mr. Simpson. Do you feel okay? Jewish? Oh, no, I'm not Jewish.
jokes.setup: Charity worker asks if Homer feels okay
jokes.punchline: Homer mishears 'Jewish' for some other word, responds inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical mishearing leads to awkward non-sequitur

jokes.text: He's gonna plotz.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks about Moe's reaction to his intoxication
jokes.punchline: Uses Yiddish word 'plotz' after just denying being Jewish
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback to the Jewish denial, shows Homer's inconsistency

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. I guess I got caught up in all the glitz and glamour.
jokes.setup: Accused of changing from being about the booze
jokes.punchline: Moe claims his dingy bar has 'glitz and glamour'
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Moe's notoriously seedy establishment

jokes.text: Uh, I don't care about the color of your skin, Lenny.
jokes.setup: Homer is extremely drunk
jokes.punchline: Makes inappropriate racial comment to Lenny out of nowhere
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing Homer's drunken lack of filter

jokes.text: Can you say the alphabet backwards? Oh, you'd like that, wouldn't you?
jokes.setup: Standard sobriety test question
jokes.punchline: Homer treats innocent question as suspicious conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Homer's paranoid drunk logic turns routine question into perceived trap

jokes.text: Hey, you want my keys? Get them now, jerks.
jokes.setup: Friends trying to prevent drunk driving
jokes.punchline: Homer presumably swallows his keys to prevent confiscation
jokes.explanation: Visual gag inferred from dialogue - Homer's extreme solution to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: Good morning, everybody. / What's for breakfast, cutie? / Homie, it's 5 p.m. We're having dinner.
jokes.setup: Homer cheerfully greets his family thinking it's morning
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals it's actually evening dinner time
jokes.explanation: Good hangover comedy showing how disoriented Homer is. Clean setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Wait. Was last night the night we set the clocks ahead eight hours?
jokes.setup: Homer confused about time of day
jokes.punchline: Suggests impossible daylight saving time adjustment of 8 hours
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - attempting to explain away his confusion with impossible scenario

jokes.text: I had to tell Mr. Burns you had violent diarrhea.
jokes.setup: Marge had to make excuse for Homer missing work
jokes.punchline: Embarrassingly graphic medical excuse
jokes.explanation: Crude humor but fits Marge's desperation for believable excuse

jokes.text: Oh, couldn't you come up with a less embarrassing lie? But you did have violent diarrhea.
jokes.setup: Homer complains about embarrassing excuse
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals it wasn't a lie at all
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure, makes the previous line even funnier in retrospect

jokes.text: Nobody open the hallway closet until I say it's okay.
jokes.setup: Following revelation about diarrhea
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer used the hallway closet as makeshift bathroom
jokes.explanation: Extends the crude humor, good economy but relies on gross-out comedy

jokes.text: It was, uh, some kind of a light rail.
jokes.setup: Asked how he got home
jokes.punchline: Homer claims he took non-existent public transportation
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate attempt to avoid admitting he drove drunk

jokes.text: I will, Dad. That's why you're my favorite.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for someone to pretend to believe him
jokes.punchline: Bart willing to enable lies to maintain favoritism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - opportunistic and morally flexible for personal gain

jokes.text: Hey, can you keep a secret? I hate disco.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu asking for secrecy
jokes.punchline: Character named Disco Stu secretly hates disco
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation that subverts everything about Disco Stu while maintaining believability

jokes.text: It's all I've talked about for so long, people think I'm a one-note guy.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu's confession about hating disco
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment that he's literally a one-note character
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the character's own limited development while staying in character

jokes.text: I had no idea Disco Stu was so complex.
jokes.setup: Following Disco Stu's revelation
jokes.punchline: Driver's genuine amazement at one-note character having depth
jokes.explanation: Extends the meta joke by having another character acknowledge the surprising depth

jokes.text: Oh, you know, I'm a guy at a place.
jokes.setup: Asked what he does for a living
jokes.punchline: Homer's maximally vague job description
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and captures Homer's inability to articulate his actual job

jokes.text: How'd you get such a crappy job? You a convict or a junkie? Little of both.
jokes.setup: Driver asks about Homer's employment background
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admits to being both a convict and junkie
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually, though somewhat out of character for Homer

jokes.text: Wife and two or three kids.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has a family
jokes.punchline: Homer uncertain about his own number of children
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inattention to important family details

jokes.text: Pretending not to remember so you could surprise us.
jokes.setup: Homer's vague family description on TV
jokes.punchline: Marge optimistically misinterprets Homer's genuine confusion
jokes.explanation: Marge's characteristic optimism and denial about Homer's flaws

jokes.text: Then there's those other days where you just wish you never got married or had kids.
jokes.setup: Homer just expressed love for his family on TV
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal into brutal honesty about marriage/parenthood regrets
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that's both funny and uncomfortable, setup by the previous sweet moment

jokes.text: Dreams of being a rock star, or a photographer for Playboy
jokes.setup: Homer describing his lost dreams
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposes artistic aspiration with juvenile sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer mixing of legitimate and ridiculous aspirations

jokes.text: Then, bam, some babe gets her claws in you
jokes.setup: Homer describing how life went wrong
jokes.punchline: Describes meeting Marge in predatory terms while she watches
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Homer's insensitive description while family watches

jokes.text: Boom, you got a bunch of kids that always needs love.
jokes.setup: Continuing Homer's rant about life going wrong
jokes.punchline: Describes children's need for love as a burden
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parenting responsibilities, matches Homer's character

jokes.text: Marriage is like a coffin and each kid is another nail.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to clarify his earlier comments
jokes.punchline: Delivers an even darker metaphor about marriage and children
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark metaphor with perfect construction and maximum economy - highly quotable

jokes.text: But as coffins go... Please don't say anymore.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to continue his coffin metaphor
jokes.punchline: Marge desperately cutting him off before he makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and character reaction, extends the coffin joke effectively

jokes.text: A place where you'll never be bothered by your family again.
jokes.setup: Marge driving Homer somewhere mysterious after his TV rant
jokes.punchline: Sounds ominous like she's taking him to be murdered
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on murder tropes before the reveal

jokes.text: Rock 'n' Roll Fantasy Camp
jokes.setup: Built up threat of abandonment
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a gift
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection payoff, though the setup was stronger than the reveal

jokes.text: For once in my life, I'm confused.
jokes.setup: Homer getting a nice surprise
jokes.punchline: Claims confusion is rare when he's constantly confused
jokes.explanation: Great irony since Homer is perpetually confused, perfect character moment

jokes.text: Even though you knew I ratted you out to the IRS... you never busted me. You what?
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Homer's tolerance during heartfelt moment
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals her betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing ruins heartfelt moment with accidental confession

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Oh, my God. Oh, my God. It's Mick Jagger and Keith Richards.
jokes.setup: Homer meeting his rock heroes
jokes.punchline: Triple repetition showing extreme excitement
jokes.explanation: Standard fanboy reaction, not particularly clever but fits Homer

jokes.text: Without the lawsuits and STDs.
jokes.setup: Describing rock and roll lifestyle experience
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions the dark side of rock stardom
jokes.explanation: Good economy and dark humor about rock star reality

jokes.text: Rule number two, no outside food. Oh, what a gyp.
jokes.setup: Keith says there are no rules, then immediately gives rule two
jokes.punchline: Homer's disappointment about food restriction over rock rules
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities show through - more concerned with food than rock freedom

jokes.text: Not today, I got a ton of paperwork to catch up on.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Mick to teach his strut
jokes.punchline: Rock legend worried about mundane office work
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between rock god image and boring business reality

jokes.text: I even took some pills I found on the floor.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's so excited
jokes.punchline: Admits to taking random floor drugs
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's reckless drug consumption, fits character

jokes.text: But he's just like you or me or Jesus over there.
jokes.setup: Homer praising Mick's relatability
jokes.punchline: Casually includes Jesus as fellow camp attendee
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with perfect economy, unexpected religious reference

jokes.text: Out of my way, nerdlinger.
jokes.setup: Competition for guitars
jokes.punchline: Adult using childish insult at rock camp
jokes.explanation: Weak insult humor, not particularly creative or character-specific

jokes.text: Did you hear that, people? Apu asked about crotch stuffing.
jokes.setup: Apu asking about masculine accentuation
jokes.punchline: Public embarrassment about private question
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from public embarrassment, fits Apu's character

jokes.text: Now, I don't do it. Kenny Loggins does. I trusted you!
jokes.setup: Kravitz denying crotch stuffing
jokes.punchline: Randomly throws Kenny Loggins under the bus, prompting betrayed response
jokes.explanation: Unexpected celebrity call-out with immediate betrayed response

jokes.text: We've gotta start using a cheaper oatmeal.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently having flaming guitar accident
jokes.punchline: Random complaint about oatmeal costs
jokes.explanation: Random Homer non-sequitur, good economy but unclear context

jokes.text: Look, everyone, Homer's got it. I'm so hot for me
jokes.setup: Homer learning rock moves
jokes.punchline: Homer's narcissistic song about himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-obsession comes through in improvised lyrics

jokes.text: When you said it in Springfield last year, you didn't mean it? Yeah, sure I did. But only because Springfield really is the wildest town in the whole damn world.
jokes.setup: Keith teaching about saying every town is wildest
jokes.punchline: Homer's earnest belief that Springfield actually is the wildest
jokes.explanation: Homer's hometown pride and inability to grasp the concept

jokes.text: She's worried about the state of public schools... She prays that our schools don't run out of chalk
jokes.setup: Writing generic rock song
jokes.punchline: Mixing rock clichés with boring civic concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of rock energy with mundane school board concerns

jokes.text: They're bloody cardboard. We know.
jokes.setup: Escaping from groupies
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're fake but continue anyway
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about the artificial nature of the fantasy camp experience

jokes.text: Really? You really think I'm better than you?
jokes.setup: Mick compliments Homer's playing
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriately competitive response to politeness
jokes.explanation: Homer's competitive nature and inability to read social cues

jokes.text: It came with a pick.
jokes.setup: Guitar being confiscated
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic additional detail to save the guitar
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate attempt to save his guitar with irrelevant detail

jokes.text: I was so close to being a rock star but now, there's a chance it might not happen.
jokes.setup: Homer lamenting end of rock camp
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about his realistic chances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer delusion and understatement about his impossible dream

jokes.text: But I like it.
jokes.setup: Marge says 'it's only rock 'n' roll'
jokes.punchline: Homer completes the Rolling Stones lyric
jokes.explanation: Simple completion of famous lyric, good economy but not highly original

jokes.text: It's like you're Kid Rock and I'm Pamela Anderson Lee Rock.
jokes.setup: Marge proud of Homer's performance
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at contemporary rock couple comparison
jokes.explanation: Marge trying too hard to be relevant with rock references

jokes.text: Wait. You misspelled 'guitar hero.'
jokes.setup: Homer gets official tour jacket
jokes.punchline: Jacket says 'roadie' but Homer thinks it's misspelled
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusion about his role extends to misreading his actual job

jokes.text: You're testing my love for you, You're checking to see if I'm true
jokes.setup: Homer supposed to just say 'test'
jokes.punchline: Improvises entire song from microphone testing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant improvisation turning mundane sound check into heartfelt song

jokes.text: What am I, the lost and found?
jokes.setup: Asked about Tom Petty's missing toe
jokes.punchline: Homer's irritated response to keeping track of body parts
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to absurd question about missing body parts

jokes.text: You're supposed to be reckless and destructive and be celebrated for behavior that would land normal people in jail.
jokes.setup: Rock stars apologizing for bad behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's astute observation about rock star privilege
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly insightful social commentary from Homer about celebrity culture

jokes.text: Rock 'n' roll! Woo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Homer in school zone
jokes.punchline: Uses rock camp experience to rebel against traffic laws
jokes.explanation: Homer applying rock rebellion to mundane traffic situation

tmdb_overview: A drunken Homer appears on the show Taxicab Conversations and rants about how awful his life as a husband and father is. Although his family is at first outraged, they realise that they do somewhat burden him and decide to make up for it by sending him to a Rock 'n Roll Fantasy Camp where he mingles with rock stars, including Mick Jagger and Keith Richards. The rock stars ask Homer to help out at a concert and, expecting that he will be allowed to perform, he accepts. Actually, they want him to test the microphones; however, a humiliated Homer grabs a guitar and starts playing.
